summary: Tested Gloss Next.js 14 app end-to-end: 5/5 backend API tests pass; frontend flows (landing, auth gates on 5 private routes, authed /scan, /library, /review 3-word full flow with grading + session-summary, /stats streak+milestones, /subscribe graceful Razorpay failure) all verified. All required data-testids present and integrations working.
critical_code_review_comments: AuthProvider client-fetches /api/auth/me on mount, so trial-badge and bottom-nav (both gated on user/sub) only render after that fetch resolves. Consider server-rendering initial user state in layout to remove UI flicker and simplify tests.

Seed script is not fully idempotent for words: `if (existing) continue` preserves prior review state, so re-seeding does not reset word statuses (e.g., ephemeral loses 'new' after any review). If deterministic test data is required, reset review rows on re-seed.
/api/auth/me returns 401 with body {ok:false} (matches spec), but response has no 'error' field for clients that expect one — minor consistency nit.
Razorpay short-circuit path in /api/subscribe correctly surfaces 'Payments are being set up. Please try again shortly.' via data-testid=subscribe-error — graceful and user-friendly.
test_credentials: Cookie session_token=test_session_gloss_demo for user demo@gloss.app (seeded via `node scripts/seed-test-user.mjs`). Note: the logout API DOES invalidate the DB session, so re-seed after any logout test before running frontend tests.
seed_data_creation: Used existing scripts/seed-test-user.mjs. Re-seeded once mid-run after logout invalidated the session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1) Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 5 tests, all passing. The logout test invalidates the DB session; re-seed with `node /app/scripts/seed-test-user.mjs` before browser tests. 2) When browser-testing authed routes, add cookie BEFORE navigation and prefer wait_for_selector on an auth-gated element (e.g., trial-badge) rather than a static element, because AuthProvider does a client-side /api/auth/me fetch that gates the nav/badge rendering. 3) Word status badges use CSS uppercase — inner_text will return 'NEW'/'LEARNING'/'LEARNED' in uppercase. 4) The Razorpay path is intentionally short-circuited in this env (RAZORPAY_KEY_ID unset) and surfaces 'Payments are being set up. Please try again shortly.' via data-testid=subscribe-error — this is expected behavior. 5) /api/define uses the real Groq API and takes ~1-2s.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Initial browser-test failures were all test-side issues: (a) cookie added after private-route navigation, (b) not waiting for AuthProvider's client-side fetch before checking trial-badge/bottom-nav, (c) checking word-status labels via loose substring match. After correcting the test approach, every required flow passed.
